CYP2D6.name: CYP2D6
CYP2D6.description: Major drug-metabolizing enzyme, processes ~25% of all medications

poor_metabolizer.description: Very slow drug metabolism; medications may accumulate to toxic levels

intermediate_metabolizer.description: Below average drug metabolism; may need dose adjustment

normal_metabolizer.description: Normal drug metabolism; standard dosing expected to be effective

ultra_rapid_metabolizer.description: Very fast drug metabolism; standard doses may be ineffective or cause toxicity with prodrugs

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Avoid codeine. Use alternative analgesics not metabolized by CYP2D6 (e.g., morphine, non-opioids)
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Use codeine with caution at reduced dose. Monitor for reduced efficacy
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Avoid codeine. Risk of life-threatening toxicity due to rapid conversion to morphine

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Avoid tramadol. Use alternative analgesic due to lack of efficacy
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Use tramadol with caution. Consider reduced dose
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Avoid tramadol. Risk of respiratory depression due to rapid O-demethylation

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Avoid tamoxifen. Consider aromatase inhibitor or alternative endocrine therapy
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Consider alternative endocrine therapy or higher tamoxifen dose (40 mg/day)

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Consider 50% dose reduction or use alternative SSRI not metabolized by CYP2D6 (e.g., sertraline, citalopram)
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Select alternative SSRI not metabolized by CYP2D6. Fluoxetine may be ineffective

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Consider 50% dose reduction or use alternative SSRI not metabolized by CYP2D6
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Select alternative SSRI not metabolized by CYP2D6

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Consider 50% dose reduction or switch to desvenlafaxine (active metabolite)
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Consider titrating to higher dose or select alternative medication

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Avoid tricyclic antidepressant or reduce dose by 50%. Use therapeutic drug monitoring
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Avoid tricyclic antidepressant. Select alternative not metabolized by CYP2D6

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce dose by 50%. Use therapeutic drug monitoring
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Avoid nortriptyline. Select alternative not metabolized by CYP2D6

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Select alternative antiemetic (e.g., granisetron). Ondansetron may have reduced efficacy
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Select alternative antiemetic not metabolized by CYP2D6

CYP2C19.name: CYP2C19
CYP2C19.description: Drug-metabolizing enzyme critical for antiplatelet therapy and proton pump inhibitors

poor_metabolizer.description: Very slow CYP2C19 metabolism; clopidogrel is ineffective

intermediate_metabolizer.description: Reduced CYP2C19 metabolism; clopidogrel efficacy may be reduced

normal_metabolizer.description: Normal CYP2C19 metabolism; standard drug response expected

ultra_rapid_metabolizer.description: Very fast CYP2C19 metabolism; PPIs may be less effective

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Use alternative antiplatelet agent (e.g., prasugrel, ticagrelor). Clopidogrel is a prodrug requiring CYP2C19 activation
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Use alternative antiplatelet agent (e.g., prasugrel, ticagrelor) if no contraindications

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce dose by 50%. Increased risk of side effects due to elevated drug levels
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Increase dose by 100% for H. pylori eradication. Standard dose may be insufficient

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce dose by 50%. Monitor for adverse effects
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Consider dose increase for adequate acid suppression

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Consider 50% dose reduction. Maximum dose 10 mg/day
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Select alternative SSRI or titrate to higher dose with monitoring

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Consider 50% dose reduction or select alternative SSRI
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Titrate to higher dose or select alternative medication

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Use standard dose but monitor trough levels closely. Risk of supratherapeutic levels
recommendation_by_status.ultra_rapid_metabolizer: Use alternative antifungal agent. Voriconazole may be subtherapeutic

CYP2C9.name: CYP2C9
CYP2C9.description: Drug-metabolizing enzyme important for warfarin and NSAID metabolism

poor_metabolizer.description: Very slow CYP2C9 metabolism; significant warfarin dose reduction needed

intermediate_metabolizer.description: Reduced CYP2C9 metabolism; moderate warfarin dose adjustment may be needed

normal_metabolizer.description: Normal CYP2C9 metabolism; standard warfarin dosing

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce warfarin dose by 50-80%. Use pharmacogenomic dosing algorithm. Increased bleeding risk
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce warfarin dose by 20-40%. Use pharmacogenomic dosing algorithm

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce phenytoin dose by 50%. Use therapeutic drug monitoring
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce phenytoin dose by 25%. Use therapeutic drug monitoring

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce dose by 50% or use alternative NSAID. Increased GI and cardiovascular risk
Reduce dose by 50% or use alternative NSAID

CYP3A5.name: CYP3A5
CYP3A5.description: Drug-metabolizing enzyme important for tacrolimus dosing in transplant patients

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Use standard initial dose (0.15 mg/kg/day). Non-expressors need lower doses
recommendation_by_status.normal_metabolizer: Increase starting dose to 0.3 mg/kg/day. Expressors metabolize tacrolimus more rapidly

CYP1A2.name: CYP1A2
CYP1A2.description: Drug-metabolizing enzyme; metabolizes caffeine, theophylline, and some antipsychotics

slow_metabolizer.description: Slow caffeine metabolism; higher sensitivity to caffeine

rapid_metabolizer.description: Rapid caffeine metabolism; reduced caffeine sensitivity

recommendation_by_status.slow_metabolizer: Slow caffeine metabolizer. Higher cardiovascular risk with high caffeine intake (>3 cups/day)

DPYD.name: DPYD
DPYD.description: Dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase; rate-limiting enzyme for fluoropyrimidine catabolism

intermediate_metabolizer.description: Partial DPYD deficiency; fluoropyrimidine dose reduction required

normal_metabolizer.description: Normal DPYD activity; standard fluoropyrimidine dosing

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: AVOID fluorouracil. Use alternative non-fluoropyrimidine chemotherapy. DPYD deficiency causes potentially fatal toxicity
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce fluorouracil dose by 50%. Monitor for toxicity closely

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: AVOID capecitabine. Use alternative non-fluoropyrimidine chemotherapy
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce capecitabine dose by 50%. Monitor for toxicity closely

TPMT.name: TPMT
TPMT.description: Thiopurine S-methyltransferase; metabolizes thiopurine drugs used in autoimmune diseases and cancer

poor_metabolizer.description: TPMT deficiency; thiopurines cause life-threatening myelosuppression

intermediate_metabolizer.description: Reduced TPMT activity; thiopurine dose reduction required

normal_metabolizer.description: Normal TPMT activity; standard thiopurine dosing

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce azathioprine dose by 90% or use alternative agent. Weekly CBC monitoring required
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce azathioprine dose by 30-70%. Monitor CBC weekly for first month

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce mercaptopurine dose by 90% and give 3 times per week. Monitor CBC closely
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce mercaptopurine dose by 30-70%. Monitor CBC weekly

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce thioguanine dose by 90%. Consider alternative therapy
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce thioguanine dose by 30-70%. Monitor CBC closely

NUDT15.name: NUDT15
NUDT15.description: Nucleoside diphosphate-linked moiety X-type motif 15; hydrolyzes thiopurine metabolites

poor_metabolizer.description: NUDT15 deficiency; thiopurines cause severe myelosuppression

intermediate_metabolizer.description: Reduced NUDT15 activity; thiopurine dose reduction required

normal_metabolizer.description: Normal NUDT15 activity; standard thiopurine dosing

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce azathioprine dose by 90% or avoid. Monitor for severe leukopenia
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce azathioprine dose by 25-50%. Monitor CBC closely

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Reduce mercaptopurine dose by 90%. Consider alternative therapy
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Reduce mercaptopurine dose by 25-50%. Monitor CBC

SLCO1B1.name: SLCO1B1
SLCO1B1.description: Solute carrier organic anion transporter; mediates hepatic uptake of statins

poor_function.description: Significantly reduced SLCO1B1 transporter function; high myopathy risk with statins

decreased_function.description: Moderately reduced SLCO1B1 function; increased myopathy risk

normal_function.description: Normal SLCO1B1 function; standard statin dosing

recommendation_by_status.poor_function: Use alternative statin (rosuvastatin, pravastatin, fluvastatin). Avoid simvastatin >20mg - high myopathy risk
recommendation_by_status.decreased_function: Use lower simvastatin dose (max 20mg) or switch to alternative statin

recommendation_by_status.poor_function: Prescribe lower dose atorvastatin or use alternative statin. Monitor for myopathy
recommendation_by_status.decreased_function: Use with caution. Prescribe lower dose if possible

recommendation_by_status.poor_function: Prescribe max 20mg rosuvastatin. Lower myopathy risk vs simvastatin
recommendation_by_status.decreased_function: Use standard dosing with monitoring for myopathy symptoms

VKORC1.name: VKORC1
VKORC1.description: Vitamin K epoxide reductase complex subunit 1; target of warfarin anticoagulation

low_warfarin_sensitivity.description: Normal VKORC1 expression; standard warfarin dose

intermediate_warfarin_sensitivity.description: Reduced VKORC1 expression; lower warfarin dose needed

high_warfarin_sensitivity.description: Very low VKORC1 expression; significantly reduced warfarin dose required

recommendation_by_status.high_warfarin_sensitivity: Reduce warfarin dose by 25-50% from standard. Use pharmacogenomic dosing algorithm incorporating CYP2C9 genotype
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_warfarin_sensitivity: Reduce warfarin dose by 15-30%. Monitor INR closely

HLA-B.name: HLA-B
HLA-B.description: Human leukocyte antigen B; specific alleles associated with severe drug hypersensitivity reactions

positive.description: Carrier of high-risk HLA-B allele; risk of severe hypersensitivity reaction

negative.description: No high-risk HLA-B allele detected; standard prescribing

recommendation_by_status.positive: AVOID abacavir. HLA-B*57:01 positive patients have 50% risk of potentially fatal hypersensitivity reaction
AVOID carbamazepine. HLA-B*15:02 positive patients at risk for Stevens-Johnson Syndrome/Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis
AVOID allopurinol. HLA-B*58:01 positive patients at risk for severe cutaneous adverse reactions

UGT1A1.name: UGT1A1
UGT1A1.description: UDP glucuronosyltransferase 1A1; glucuronidation enzyme important for irinotecan and atazanavir metabolism

poor_metabolizer.description: UGT1A1 deficiency (Gilbert syndrome genotype); reduced drug glucuronidation

intermediate_metabolizer.description: Intermediate UGT1A1 activity; may need dose adjustment for some drugs

normal_metabolizer.description: Normal UGT1A1 activity; standard drug dosing

recommendation_by_status.poor_metabolizer: Consider alternative antiretroviral if patient develops jaundice. UGT1A1*28/*28 increases risk of hyperbilirubinemia
Reduce irinotecan dose by 30%. UGT1A1*28/*28 increases risk of severe neutropenia and diarrhea
recommendation_by_status.intermediate_metabolizer: Use standard irinotecan dose with close monitoring for toxicity
